tier_legend.1: Government filings, court records, legislation, Elections Alberta, CRTC, AG, Hansard, party primary statements
tier_legend.2: Named journalist at established outlet with corrections policy
tier_legend.4: Aggregator / finding tool — follow through to Tier 1 or 2, never cite directly
tier_legend.opinion: Tier-2 outlet, Opinion/Analysis section — label in UI, corroborate factual claims separately

meta.verified_note: verified:true = URL confirmed live and content-matched. verified:false = real event, source not yet confirmed — do not publish.
meta.outlet_id_note: outlet_id is the join key to outlet-database.json. Use kebab-case exactly.
meta.display_rule: Never show party affiliation in event titles. Institutional role only. Party affiliation is tier-2 expanded view.
meta.schema_notes: v0.6.0: Added R-055 (expense reversal/CP), R-056 (Kenney earplugs/CTV), R-057 (ATA 'good deal'/CP), R-058 (Johnson MLA record/Legislature T1), R-059 (carbon MOU/CBC), R-060 (3000 teachers/French CBC), R-061 (separatist leadership/CBC), R-062 (strike timeline/Airdrie T3), R-063 (Johnson 'final'/Bennett CP). R-055 resolves a dangling reference on EVT-037. | v0.7.0: Added R-064 to R-080 (stubs for harvested events). Many are pending-verification — URLs sourced from per-year and Evidence-Part files, not yet hand-checked.

references.title: Women's economic recovery — Associate Minister Issik statement
references.notes: Primary source for SD-012. Issik claims unemployment 'lowest since before pandemic' and women 'leading nation.' Both contradicted by StatsCan LFS Dec 2021.

references.title: Labour Force Survey, December 2021
references.notes: Tier 1 primary for SD-012 DID side. AB female unemployment 7.6% Dec 2021 (up from 7.3% Nov; above 6.9% pre-pandemic Feb 2020). Women received 12.6% of new jobs and 100% of new part-time jobs. URL needs HTTP verification.

references.title: What Whitney Issik left out about women workers
references.author: Kim Siever
references.notes: FINDING TOOL ONLY — Tier 4. Use to locate StatsCan table locations (R-041). Do not cite directly.

references.title: Alberta is calling and professional racialized women are answering — some, to a rude awakening
references.author: Thandiwe Konguavi
references.notes: Context for EVT-031. Edmonton Black women median income $34,800 vs men $47,200. Gap between 'Alberta is Calling' messaging and racialized women's lived reality.

references.title: Danielle Smith believes Alberta UCP policies on trans youth are 'supportive'
references.notes: Primary SAID source for SD-013. Smith: 'supportive,' 'consulted very broadly' (refused to name who). Expert rebuttal: Travers (SFU) + Brennan (U of T) on puberty blockers and suicide risk. Bottom surgery already 18+ Canada-wide — announcement addressed non-problem.

references.title: Alberta legislation on transgender youth, student pronouns and sex education set to become law
references.author: Andrew Jeffrey
references.notes: DID source for SD-013. Education Amendment Act 2024, Health Statutes Amendment Act, Fairness and Safety in Sport Act pass. Egale Canada + Skipping Stone Foundation file Charter challenge. Smith declines to confirm notwithstanding clause use if struck down.

references.title: Calgary to wind down Green Line LRT project with costs estimated at $2.1B
references.author: Adam MacVicar
references.notes: Primary source for SD-014 and EVT-034. Province withdrew $1.53B via Dreeshen letter. City council 10-5 to terminate. $850M wind-down + $1.3B spent = $2.1B. Dreeshen: won't cover wind-down, blames 'decade-long mismanagement.'

references.title: Green Line LRT project undergoes upheaval
references.notes: FINDING TOOL / context (Tier 3). ~$850M wind-down figure is one of three figures presented on EVT-034 (provincial $1.53B / total $2.1B / wind-down ~$850M). UCP cut Green Line contribution 86% in 2019; passed 90-day withdrawal legislation.

references.title: Alberta Referendum 2026 — Official FAQ (immigration and constitutional questions)
references.notes: Government primary source for the Oct 19 2026 referendum: nine questions on immigration and constitutional reform. Anchors rescoped EVT-029. Date is approximate (site live in 2026; confirm exact publication). Government framing — present as the government's stated position, not neutral fact.

references.title: Service Alberta Minister Dale Nally breached election law, investigation finds
references.author: Daniela Germano (The Canadian Press)
references.notes: Anchor for EVT-035. Elections Alberta found Nally accessed confidential List of Electors 'for a purpose not authorized by the Election Act'; no penalty, advice given. Recall petition against Nally failed (~2,600 of 15,700 signatures). Related to but distinct from Centurion Project.

references.title: Elections Alberta issues cease-and-desist letters over voter info breach, privacy commissioner launches probe
references.author: Wallis Snowdon
references.notes: FACTUAL ANCHOR for EVT-028 (Centurion Project). Court of King's Bench temporary injunction granted ~Apr 30 2026; database of ~2.9M voters taken down; cease-and-desist to 568 people (23 who received list + 545 who accessed); permanent-injunction hearing set late July; privacy commissioner (McLeod) investigating under PIPA s.36(1)(a); RCMP parallel investigation. List sourced from Republican Party of Alberta's legitimately-issued copy, used by Centurion (third-party advertiser) in unauthorized manner. Justice Thomas Rothwell.

references.title: Alberta Separatist Group's Controversial Voter ID App Has Links to US Ambassador, MAGA Influencers and Wealthy Michigan Republicans
references.notes: Advocacy non-profit (self-describes covering right-wing extremism) — label lean in UI; quotes are on-record. Foreign-influence angle: Centurion's app modelled on/linked to Michigan-based 10xVotes; US Ambassador Hoekstra previously promoted 10xVotes (denies involvement in Alberta use). Supports STAGED cluster EVT-NEW-centurion-foreign, not yet an EVT record.

references.title: The Centurion Project Scandal Ensnares the UCP
references.author: David Climenhaga
references.notes: OPINION/ANALYSIS — label in UI, corroborate facts separately. Covers NDP video evidence of UCP caucus-staff attendance at Apr 16 Centurion training meeting; Kenney's personal info displayed; Smith's 'implausible deniability'. Context for EVT-028; the UCP-meeting/Kenney-doxxing strand is STAGED (EVT-NEW-centurion-ucp-link).

references.title: What Is the Centurion Project — and Why Did They Have Alberta's Voter Data?
references.notes: FINDING TOOL ONLY (Substack, tier 4 — do not cite directly). Strong chain-of-custody summary and network mapping (Parker/TBA, Sylvestre/Stay Free Alberta, Davies/RPA). Use to locate the Tier 1 Elections Alberta releases (Apr 30 2026) and Tier 2 CBC/Globe/Global coverage it cites.

references.title: Massive List of Electors data breach was inevitable — the UCP's law-bending style of politics made it so
references.author: David Climenhaga
references.notes: FINDING TOOL ONLY (albertapolitics.ca = tier 4 per HANDOFF_v3, do not cite as source_ref). Opinion. Notes privacy commissioner Diane McLeod statement and the PIPA-exemption-for-parties problem; links Nally matter to broader pattern. Use to locate Tier 1/2 sources.

references.title: Alberta government reverts expense reporting rules; premier says she was unaware
references.author: Lisa Johnson
references.notes: Expense records reversal. Aug 1 policy removed $100 receipt disclosure + deleted ~500,000 entries (2012-2020). Cabinet reversal Aug 19. Smith says unaware ('lost in translation'). Already referenced by EVT-037 (this merge resolves that dangling reference).

references.title: Alberta premier hands out earplugs during debate on bill affecting union rights
references.notes: Kenney earplug incident, Bill 9 all-night debate. Anchors EVT-007. Bellefontaine/CBC Jun 20 2019 is the ideal source if locatable; this CTV/CP article is the confirmed substitute.

references.title: Province says Alberta teachers have rejected a 'good deal' as bargaining stalemate continues
references.author: Lisa Johnson
references.notes: Sept 2025 stalemate. Govt offer 12%/4yr + 3,000 teachers + $2.3B. Horner: 'good deal'. Anchors SD-016 SAID side.

references.title: Member information — Jennifer Johnson, MLA Lacombe-Ponoka, 31st Legislature
references.notes: Official record: Johnson elected, seated in UCP caucus, resigned, readmitted. Tier 1 for SD-017 DID side. outlet_id 'alberta-legislature' not yet in outlet-database.json — add at next outlet pass.

references.title: Ottawa, Alberta near deal on carbon pricing rising to $130/tonne by 2040
references.author: David Thurton, Kate McKenna
references.notes: Smith-Carney carbon deal: $130/tonne effective by 2040 under TIER. Current effective ~$45 vs headline $95. Canadian Climate Institute calls 2040 'too late'. BC Premier Eby objects. Announcement pending.

references.title: The Alberta government promised 3,000 more teachers. Some say that's misleading.
references.author: Janet French
references.notes: 3,000-teacher 'commitment' was pre-existing: $750M in March 2025 budget before strike. Anchors SD-016 DID side. Also confirms Back to School Act = Oct 27, s.33 invoked, 89.5% vote-down.

references.title: Alberta separatist leader planning to challenge Danielle Smith's leadership or change referendum question
references.author: Jason Markusoff
references.notes: Sylvestre organizing UCP leadership review or referendum-question change. Oct 19 2026 referendum: remain vs begin secession process. Smith pro-remain; UCP neutral. Anchors EVT-046.

references.title: Tracing the timeline of Alberta's historic teachers' strike
references.author: Kajal Dhaneshwari
references.notes: Strike dates: Oct 6 strike; Oct 7 lockout notice; Oct 9 lockout; Oct 14 ATA counter-proposal + Parent Payment Program ($30/child/day); Oct 15 govt review. Tier 3 local. outlet_id 'airdrie-city-view' not in outlet-database. Corroborate dates w/ Tier 2 where possible.

references.title: UCP candidate will not sit in caucus if elected, after comments on transgender children: Smith
